sentiment.rationale: Pronounces total judgment upon Edom for pride and violence against Judah, but closes with the promise of deliverance on mount Zion and saviors who shall judge Esau.

missingCharacters.name: Obadiah (prophet)
missingCharacters.description: Old Testament prophet who delivered a vision of judgment against Edom and deliverance for Israel.

summaries.summary: A vision against Edom for its pride and betrayal of Judah during Jerusalem's destruction; Edom will be brought low while saviors shall come up on mount Zion.

crossReferences.note: Both prophets use nearly identical language about Edom's pride in dwelling among the cliffs.
Saviors on mount Zion — a key LDS passage on vicarious temple work for the dead.

verses.reason: 'Though thou exalt thyself as the eagle... thence will I bring thee down' — a vivid image of God humbling the proud.
'Saviours shall come up on mount Zion to judge the mount of Esau' — foundational LDS verse on proxy temple ordinances.
